summary: This letter is about your Ontario Disability Support Program (ODSP) application or benefit status.
key_points: The letter confirms a decision regarding your ODSP eligibility or benefit amount.
ODSP provides monthly income support and may include health and employment supports.
If approved, your benefit amount is based on your household size, shelter costs, and other income.
action_items: Review the decision date and any conditions listed in the letter.
If you disagree with the decision, you have 30 days to request an internal review.
Contact your local ODSP office if you need clarification or want to report a change in circumstances.
deadlines: Internal review request: within 30 days of the decision date shown on the letter.

summary: This letter relates to your Employment Insurance (EI) claim with Service Canada.
key_points: The letter may confirm approval, payment details, or a decision about your EI claim.
You must complete bi-weekly reports to continue receiving EI payments.
Your benefit rate is typically 55% of your average insurable weekly earnings, up to a maximum.
action_items: Check whether your claim was approved, denied, or requires additional information.
If approved, set up direct deposit and complete your first bi-weekly report on time.
If denied, review the reason and consider requesting a reconsideration within 30 days.
deadlines: Bi-weekly reports: due every two weeks while receiving benefits.
Reconsideration request: within 30 days of the decision date.

summary: This is a Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) notice about your tax return or account.
key_points: A Notice of Assessment summarizes the results of your tax return processing.
It shows any refund owed to you or balance you need to pay.
The letter may include information about benefits like the Canada Child Benefit or GST/HST credit.
action_items: Compare the assessment to your filed tax return for accuracy.
If you owe money, note the payment due date to avoid interest charges.
Keep the notice for your records and update any benefit applications that require proof of income.
deadlines: Payment due date: shown on the notice if a balance is owed.
Objection period: 90 days from the date of the assessment to file a formal objection.

summary: This letter concerns your rent-geared-to-income (RGI) or social housing application or tenancy.
key_points: RGI housing sets your rent at approximately 30% of your household's gross monthly income.
You must report income changes to your housing provider to keep your rent accurate.
Waitlist placement or unit offers depend on local social housing availability.
action_items: Confirm whether this is an application update, waitlist notice, or unit offer.
If offered a unit, respond by the deadline or you may lose your place on the waitlist.
Gather required documents (income proof, ID) if your application is being processed.
deadlines: Unit offer response: typically 48–72 hours as stated in the letter.
Annual income review: due by the date specified by your housing provider.
